title: Copolymer and method for treatment of bacterial infection
abstract: A method of treatment of infection in a subject comprising administering to the subject a copolymer comprising an acrolein derived segment or a polyacrolein oligomer segment and a polyalkylene glycol oligomer segment, the copolymer having a molecular weight of no more than 1500 Daltons.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A method of treatment of a parenteral infection in a subject comprising administering to the subject a copolymer comprising acrolein derived segment and a polyalkylene glycol oligomer segment, the copolymer having a molecular weight of no more than 1500 Daltons. 2. A method according to claim 1 wherein the acrolein derived segment is a polyacrolein oligomer comprising two or more acrolein residues. 3. The method according to claim 1 , wherein the copolymer has a molecular weight of from 400 to 1000 Daltons. 4. The method according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the polyalkylene glycol oligomer segment has a molecular weight in the range of from 200 to 600 Daltons. 5. The method according to any one of the previous claims, wherein the polyalkylene glycol is polyethylene glycol. 6. The method according to any one of the previous claims, wherein the parenteral infection is selected from the group consisting of infections of the blood (bacteremia), meninges, lungs, urinary tract, sinuses, skin, wounds, abscesses and surgical procedures. 7. A method according to any one of the previous claims wherein the parenteral infection is an antibiotic-resistant infection. 8. A method according to any one of the previous claims wherein the parenteral infection is a multi-drug antibiotic-resistant bacterial infection. 9. The method according to any one of the previous claims, wherein the parenteral infection is selected from sepsis and bacteremia. 10. The method according to any one of the previous claims, wherein the copolymer 

 is administered locally to an infected inter-cellular and/or intra-cellular component, tissue or organ underneath the outer-protective membranes or the skin such as punctures, injections, bites, cuts, wounds, surgery, splits between skin and mucous membranes tissue or organ in an amount effective to provide local treatment of the infected tissue or organ. 1 1 . The method according to any one of the previous claims, wherein the copolymer is administered system ically. 12. The method according to any one of the previous claims, wherein the copolymer is administered by a route selected from the group consisting of oral administration, inhalation, transdermal delivery and injection. 13. The method according to any one of the previous claims, wherein the copolymer is administered by oral administration and the copolymer is of molecular weight no more than 1000. 14. The method according to any one of the previous claims, wherein the copolymer is injected into the circulatory system or bloodstream. 15. The method according to any one of the previous claims, wherein the copolymer is administered as an aqueous solution comprising in the range of from 0.01 % by weight to 20% by weight of the of the composition. 16. The method according to any one of claims 9 to 10, wherein the copolymer is administered orally in the form of a tablet, caplet, syrup or liquid. 17. The method according to any one of the previous claims wherein the copolymer is administered systemically at a dose in the range of from 1 mg to 1000mg per kilogram of bodyweight per day, or preferably from 10 mg to 500mg per kilogram of bodyweight per day. 18. The method according to any one of the previous claims, wherein the infection is a bacterial infection selected from the group consisting of Proteus spp, Serratia 

 spp, Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Neisseria meningitidis, Escherichia coli, Klebsiella pneumoniae, Staphylococcus aureus, coagulase-negative Staphylococcus spp, Streptococcus pyogenes, Streptococcus pneumoniae, Enterococcus spp. 19. The method according to any one of the previous claims wherein the parenteral infection is caused by a bacterium resistant to at least one antibiotic. 20. Use of a copolymer comprising an polyacrolein oligomer segment and polyalkylene glycol oligomer segment, in manufacture of a medicament for treatment of a parenteral infection in a subject wherein the copolymer is for oral or parenteral administration to the subject, the copolymer having a molecular weight of no more than 1500 Daltons, preferably no more than 1000 Daltons. 21 . The use according to claim 20 wherein the molecular weight of the polyalkylene glycol is in the range of from 200 to 600 Daltons. 22. A process for preparation of a copolymer comprising an acrolein derived segment and polyalkylene glycol oligomer comprising polymerizing acrolein under conditions of alkaline catalysis at a pH no more than 12 in an aqueous solution comprising at least 20% w/w water and polyalkylene glycol oligomer in a weight ratio of polyalkylene glycol /acrolein of at least 4, preferably at least 10. 23. The process according to claim 22 wherein the molecular weight of the polyalkylene glycol is from 200 to 600 Daltons. 24. The process according to claim 22 or claim 23 comprising adding an aqueous solution of the acrolein having an acrolein concentration of no more than 50%w/w, to an aqueous solution of polyethylene glycol comprising at least 10% w/w water and having a pH of no more than 12.0. 25. The process according to any one of claims 22 to 24 wherein the acrolein is added as an aqueous solution to an aqueous solution of polyethylene glycol of 

 pH 9 to 1 1 . 26. A process according to any one claims 22 to 25 comprising the following steps: providing a mildly basic (preferably of pH no more than 12.0; more preferably of pH 9 to 1 1 ) aqueous solution of a polyalkylene glycol (preferably polyethylene glycol of molecular weight in the range of from 200 to 600 Daltons); stirring the mildly basic solution vigorously to entrain air; adding (preferably slowly over a period such as at least 2 minutes, more preferably at least 5 minutes) acrolein as an aqueous solution of concentration no more than 50 % w/w of the acrolein aqueous solution (usually containing preservative); maintaining the reaction temperature in the range of from 10Â°C to 40Â°C; and once the acrolein monomer has been consumed, adding acid to provide a pH less than 9 and preferably no more than 8. 27. The process according to claim 26 wherein the acrolein is added to the aqueous polyalkylene glycol as an aqueous solution of concentration in the range of from 10% to 30% by weight acrolein monomer based on the weight of the aqueous acrolein solution to be added to the aqueous polyalkylene glycol solution. 28. The process according to any one of claims 22 to 27, wherein the resulting copolymer has a reactive carbonyl group content (including any carboxyl- content) of less than 10%. 29. A copolymer for treatment of parenteral infection in a subject comprising a acrolein derived segment and a polyalkylene glycol oligomer segment, the copolymer having a molecular weight of no more than 1500 Daltons. 30. A copolymer according to claim 29 wherein the acrolein derived segment is a polyacrolein oligomer 31 . The copolymer according to claim 29 or 30, wherein the copolymer has a molecular weight of from 400 to 1000 Daltons. 32. The copolymer according to any one of claims 29 to 31 , wherein the polyalkylene glycol oligomer segment has a molecular weight in the range of from 200 to 600 Daltons. 33. The copolymer according to any one of claims 29 to 32, wherein the polyalkylene glycol is polyethylene glycol. 34. The copolymer of any one of claims 29 to 33 formed by polymerizing acrolein under conditions of alkaline catalysis at a pH no more than 12.0 in an aqueous solution comprising at least 20% w/w water and polyalkylene glycol oligomer in a weight ratio of polyalkylene glycol /acrolein of at least 4, preferably at least 10.
